title: A Quiet Place: Day One Box Office Update
text: The horror-sci-fi spinoff has accumulated $241.4 million worldwide against its $67 million budget. The film stars Lupita Nyong'o and Joseph Quinn in this prequel exploring the origins of the sound-sensitive aliens introduced in the previous two films. In its fourth weekend, the movie earned $6.1 million domestically, maintaining fifth place at the box office. The film has grossed approximately $123.4 million in the U.S., approaching the original installment's domestic total. The earnings represented a less than fifty percent drop from last weekend. Across 67 markets, Day One brought in $5.2 million, bringing the international tally to $113.8 million. Mexico led international markets with $12.7 million total, followed by strong performances in the United Kingdom, Australia, and France. Day One is tracking similarly to previous entries. It sits closer to A Quiet Place Part II's $297.4 million worldwide total, though surpassing the original film's $341 million remains unlikely. Michael Sarnoski directed this installment the first Quiet Place film not helmed by John Krasinski, who produced instead.
